issues.title: Undocumented limitation when using Intelligent Terminal with Copilot on Windows 11
issues.next_action_reason: Blocked community reporter supplied logs revealing root cause (no CLI on PATH); explain + acknowledge doc gap.
issues.suggested_reply: @Sybelius thank you for the logs - they show the key detail: `_IsAgentInstalled: copilot/claude/codex/gemini NOT found on PATH`. Intelligent Terminal doesn't ship its own model; it drives an agent CLI you install. With no CLI on PATH it falls back to the built-in Copilot path, which is what surfaces the "requires enterprise/org policy" message. Please install and sign into an agent CLI (e.g. GitHub Copilot CLI or Claude Code) so it's on PATH, then relaunch. We'll also improve the docs/error to make this explicit - tracking that here.

Two separate things here: (1) On Windows today you can already point Intelligent Terminal at any ACP-speaking CLI — including OpenCode — by setting `acpAgent` to `custom:<command>` (or `acpCustomCommand`) in settings.json, so it runs as a custom agent on the Windows host. issues.next_action_reason: Community bug-fix, CI green, mergeStateStatus=BLOCKED + reviewDecision=REVIEW_REQUIRED, only the Copilot bot has commented — needs a human reviewer assigned/approved. High severity (breaks all debug builds).
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @DDKinger — nice catch. This lines up: `debug_assert()` runs the clap command-tree validation inside `Cli::parse()`, which every debug `wta.exe` (including the `wta-master` the C++ side spawns) hits at startup, so the `-h` collision panics before the pane ever serves. Could you confirm `wta split-pane --help` still shows `--horizontal` and `-h` maps to help after the change?

issues.title: Clipboard integration on libweston may cause race condition and crash the weston process
issues.next_action_reason: Reproducible Weston crash with a community-supplied root cause and open upstream fix PR (weston-mirror#166) that has gone unacknowledged; acknowledge, label clipboard-integration/weston-upstream-bug, and review the upstream PR.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @tumugin for the precise trace, and @abjfh30 for the root-cause analysis and the upstream fix in microsoft/weston-mirror#166. To summarize for anyone following: rapid clipboard copies race in `clipboard_data_source_send()` — a new request replaces `source->data_source_fd` while the old `transfer_event_source` is still registered against the previous fd, so when the stale callback fires it trips `assert(source->data_source_fd == fd)` at libweston/backend-rdp/rdpclip.c:967 and takes down Weston. We'll review weston-mirror#166; keeping the active transfer intact and closing the overlapping request fd looks like the right shape (accepting that an overlapping paste may EOF and need a retry). Labeling this a Weston/clipboard-integration bug and tracking the upstream PR. @abjfh30 — did you see any case where the retry path itself deadlocks, or does the overlapping paste always recover cleanly?
